attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: accessible two-adic exponent cycle plus positive finite-prefix Hensel lifts
attempts.attempt: Improve the certified logarithmic Mersenne delay coefficient beyond the positive-reference limit 1/2.

two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.delay_definition: D(k)=min{t>=0: A^(k+t)(2^k-1)<2^k-1}, with infinity if no such t exists.

two_adic_cycle.natural_number_guard: kappa and the negative cycle are 2-adic proof devices only; every k_H is a positive ordinary exponent.

two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.explicit_sequence: For each H, let d_H=1+sum of the first H symbols of 2,1,2,1,..., let r_H be the unique odd residue modulo 2^(d_H-1) with 3^r_H=-13 mod 2^(d_H+1), and set k_H=r_H+2^(d_H-1).
two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.statement: For every H>=2, D(k_H)>H>(2/3)*log2(k_H)-1. Consequently every c*log2(k) post-expansion Mersenne descent window with fixed c<2/3 fails on infinitely many exponents.
proof_chain: Odd powers of 3 modulo 2^Q are exactly the residues congruent to 3 modulo 8; since -13=3 mod 8, a unique odd exponent residue r_Q exists modulo 2^(Q-2).
The residue is constructed one bit at a time: exactly one of r and r+2^(Q-2) lifts 3^r=-13 from modulus 2^Q to 2^(Q+1).
The 2-adic post value -7 follows the exact accelerated valuation cycle 2,1 and alternates with -5.
Preserving 3^k=-13 modulo 2^(d_H+1) preserves the exact finite valuation prefix for the positive exponent k_H=r_H+2^(d_H-1).
The symbolic iterates satisfy x_t(k)=(3^(k+t)+c_t)/2^d_t, d_t follows the 2,1 word, and |c_t|<2^(2t+1).
For d_H>=4, k_H>=2^(d_H-1)+1 and the base inequality (3/2)^9>2^5 propagates to give (3/2)^k_H>2^(d_H+1).
This multiplicative margin and the affine bound force x_t(k_H)>2^k_H-1 for every 0<=t<=H, hence D(k_H)>H.
Because k_H<2^d_H and d_H<=3H/2+3/2, one obtains H>(2/3)log2(k_H)-1.

rejected_candidate_families.counteredge: D(k_H)>H>(2/3)log2(k_H)-1 on an explicit Hensel-lifted positive exponent sequence.
The positive reference tail has mean valuation 2, while the accessible -7/-5 2-adic cycle has mean 3/2 and improves the coefficient from 1/2 to 2/3.
Only finite exponent residues are lifted to positive integers; the negative 2-adic cycle itself is never classified as a natural orbit.

two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.discarded_route: Treat the TICKET83 positive reference orbit and its coefficient 1/2 as optimal, or promote the negative 2-adic cycle itself to a natural counterexample.
two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.retained_route: Classify accessible periodic 2-adic exponent cycles by mean valuation, lift only finite prefixes to positive exponents, and optimize the rigorous logarithmic delay coefficient.
two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.candidate_theorem: AccessibleCycleCoefficientSupremum: classify all periodic accelerated valuation words whose 2-adic cycle lies in the exponent image x=(3^kappa-1)/2, and determine the supremum of reciprocal mean valuation subject to exact positive-exponent lifts.
two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.equivalence_warning: A stronger finite-delay lower bound still does not imply divergence. The 2-adic cycle is a prefix generator, not a positive Collatz orbit.

literature_context.citation: Bernstein and Lagarias, The 3x+1 Conjugacy Map, Canadian Journal of Mathematics 48 (1996)
Terras, A stopping time problem on the positive integers, Acta Arithmetica 30 (1976)

two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.novelty_boundary: 2-adic cycles and prefix lifting are established ideas. PrimeProject claims only this accessible exponent-cycle construction, the explicit two-thirds logarithmic corollary, and its reproducible audit; novelty requires independent literature review.
two_adic_cycle_log_delay_audit.proof_boundary: TICKET84 proves a restricted finite-delay lower bound. It proves neither divergence nor the Collatz conjecture nor any of the other three open problems.

attempts.obstruction: Treat the TICKET83 positive reference orbit and its coefficient 1/2 as optimal, or promote the negative 2-adic cycle itself to a natural counterexample.
attempts.candidate_theorem: AccessibleCycleCoefficientSupremum: classify all periodic accelerated valuation words whose 2-adic cycle lies in the exponent image x=(3^kappa-1)/2, and determine the supremum of reciprocal mean valuation subject to exact positive-exponent lifts.
attempts.next_experiment: Build TICKET85 to enumerate accessible periodic valuation words and optimize reciprocal mean valuation.
